name: Almost La Victoria's Green Taco Sauce
recipeIngredient: 2 lbs. Anaheim or New Mexico chiles, roasted, peeled, and deseeded
1 lb. Yellow Hungarian Wax chiles, seeded and chopped
1 Serrano and jalapeno, seeded and chopped
7 lbs. Green tomatoes, sliced 1/4 inch thick
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat a large cast iron skillet to hot and toast the tomatoes and tomatillos without any oil. Toast in batches, giving each slice a nice dark brown color on both sides without burning. Remove toasted slices to a glass bowl.
2. In a blender, combine the onion, cilantro, chiles, half of the sliced tomatoes, garlic, and half of the tomatillo slices in batch sizes that fill the blender jar about halfway. Puree.
3. If any dark brown liquid collects in the bottom of the toasted tomato and tomatillo bowl, add it to the last blender load.
4. Mix the cornstarch with lime juice/vinegar in a small bowl.
5. In a large stewing pot, combine the blender loads, add the cornstarch mixture, and heat until the sauce comes to a low boil, mixing constantly.
6. Be careful not to stop stirring, as the thick sauce may erupt into hot geyser-like streams that could burn you. Allow the sauce to cool and adjust salt to taste.
7. Transfer to clean jars, filling them 3/4 full. Freeze what you can't use within a few weeks. Be careful not to fill the jars too full; they may break when frozen.
8. I made a batch of sauce last year, and it tastes just fine after a year in the freezer. The sauce keeps well in my refrigerator for at least a month.
9. Adjust heat level to your personal taste. The La Victoria sauce is mild. 3 to 4 quarts
